info.name: FlowShift API
info.description:
## FlowShift — iPaaS Migration Playbook Generator

FlowShift converts workflows between iPaaS platforms (n8n, Make, Zapier, Tray, Boomi, Workato, Celigo, Power Automate) using Claude AI. Submit a source workflow — as a file or plain-text description — and receive a detailed migration playbook plus a ready-to-import destination workflow file.

---

### Authentication

All `/api/*` endpoints require an `X-API-Key` header. The key is returned **once** when you register and is stored in the `{{api_key}}` collection variable automatically.

```
X-API-Key: {{api_key}}
```

---

### Quick start

1. **Register** — run *Register User* to create an account. `api_key` and `user_id` are saved to collection variables automatically.
2. **Submit a run** — run *Create Run (description only)* or *Create Run (with file content)*. The `run_id` is saved automatically.
3. **Poll for results** — run *Get Run* every few seconds until `status` is `completed`. The response includes the full playbook text and import file content.
4. **Browse history** — run *List User Runs* at any time to see all past migrations.

**Slack bot:** Type `/flowshift link <api-key>` in Slack after installing the app (see `SLACK_SETUP.md`). All commands — new runs, list, status — are available via `/flowshift` without touching the REST API directly.

**Lost your API key?** Use *Request Recovery Email* (enter your registered email), then click the link in the email. *Redeem Recovery Token* exchanges the token for a new API key and auto-saves it.

---

### Collection variables

| Variable | Set by | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `base_url` | You | API base URL (default: `https://flowshift-cdl.fly.dev`) |
| `api_key` | Register User (auto) | Your API key — required on all authenticated requests |
| `user_id` | Register User (auto) | Your user ID |
| `run_id` | Create Run (auto) | Most recently created run ID |

### File uploads

The API accepts file content as a plain JSON string in `fileContent` — no binary upload. Read the file in a text editor, copy all, and paste into the request body. Chrome on macOS may block programmatic file reads (`NotReadableError`) for files with emoji in the filename; the web UI handles this automatically via a paste fallback.

---

### Run status lifecycle

```
pending → processing → completed
                    ↘ failed
```

`POST /api/runs` returns `202 Accepted` with `status: pending` immediately. Claude processes the migration in the background (typically 30–60 seconds). Poll `GET /api/runs/:id` until status reaches a terminal state.

variable.description: API base URL. Change to http://localhost:8080 for local development.
Your API key. Populated automatically by the Register User test script. Store it securely — it is shown only once.
Your user ID (UUID). Populated automatically by the Register User test script.
The most recently created run ID. Populated automatically by the Create Run test scripts. Used by Get Run.
Claude model used for generation. Corresponds to the CLAUDE_MODEL env var.
Max tokens for Claude responses. Corresponds to the MAX_TOKENS env var.

item.name: Health
item.description: Liveness check. Use this to confirm the API server is reachable and the Node.js process has started successfully. No authentication required.

request.description:
Returns the service name and current server timestamp.

**Authentication:** None required.

**Use this to:**
- Verify the server is up before running other requests
- Check the server clock (useful for debugging timestamp fields in run responses)
- Confirm a fresh deployment is healthy

**Response fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `status` | string | Always `"ok"` when the process is running |
| `service` | string | Always `"flowshift-api"` |
| `timestamp` | string | ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of the server at request time |

item.name: Users
item.description:
User registration and profile management.

Users are identified by a UUID and authenticated via an API key. The API key is returned **once** at registration time and cannot be retrieved again — store it securely. If lost, you will need to register a new account.

All endpoints in this folder except *Register User* require the `X-API-Key` header.

**Recovery flow:** If a user loses their API key, `POST /recover` sends a one-time email link. `GET /recover/:token` validates the link, resets the key, and returns the new value. The old key is invalidated immediately on redemption.

exec: pm.test('api_key and user_id saved to collection variables', () => {
console.log('API key saved. Store it securely — it will not be shown again.');

request.description:
Create a new user account. Returns an `api_key` that must be included in the `X-API-Key` header on all subsequent requests.

**Authentication:** None required.

**Important:** The `api_key` is returned **only once** in this response. The built-in test script automatically saves it to the `{{api_key}}` collection variable. Store it separately in a secure location — it cannot be retrieved again.

**Request body:**

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `email` | string | ✓ | Must be a valid email address. Must be unique — registering with an existing email returns 409. |
| `name` | string | | Display name shown in the dashboard. |

**Response fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | string (UUID) | Your user ID — used in all `/api/users/:id/*` endpoints |
| `email` | string | Normalized to lowercase |
| `name` | string \| null | Display name |
| `api_key` | string (UUID) | Your API key — **shown once only** |

response.name: 400 Bad Request — missing or invalid email
409 Conflict — email already registered

request.description:
Returns the profile of the currently authenticated user based on the `X-API-Key` header. This endpoint is used by the sign-in flow to look up a user when only the API key is known (i.e., the user ID is not yet in localStorage).

**Authentication:** `X-API-Key` header required.

**Response fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | string (UUID) | User ID |
| `email` | string | Email address |
| `name` | string \| null | Display name |
| `gdrive_folder_id` | string \| null | Google Drive folder ID. Null until the first run completes with Drive enabled. |
| `gdrive_folder_url` | string \| null | Shareable Drive folder URL. Set alongside `gdrive_folder_id`. |
| `created_at` | string | ISO 8601 UTC creation timestamp |

**Note:** The `api_key` field is intentionally excluded from this response.

Fetch a user's full profile by their UUID. The authenticated user may only fetch their own profile — attempting to fetch another user's profile returns 403.

**Authentication:** `X-API-Key` header required.

**Path parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| `:id` | User UUID (stored in `{{user_id}}`) |

**Response fields:** Same as *Get Current User (me)*.

**Errors:**
- `401` — missing or invalid API key
- `403` — the API key belongs to a different user than the `:id` in the path

query.description: Number of runs to return (default: 50, max: 500)

request.description:
List runs for a user, ordered newest first. Supports pagination via `limit` and `offset` query parameters.

**Query parameters:**

| Parameter | Default | Max | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `limit` | `50` | `500` | Number of runs to return |
| `offset` | `0` | — | Pagination offset |

Returns `{ runs: [...], limit, offset }`. Summary objects only — no `playbook_text` or `import_file_content`.
Use `GET /api/runs/:id` for full run details.

**Authentication:** `X-API-Key` header required.

**Path parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| `:id` | User UUID (stored in `{{user_id}}`) |

Initiates the API key recovery flow. Sends a one-time recovery link to the provided email address.

**Authentication:** None required.

**Request body:**

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `email` | string | ✓ | The email address used when registering. |

**Security note:** The response is always `200 OK` with the same neutral message regardless of whether the email is registered. This prevents account enumeration — an attacker cannot determine which emails have accounts.

**Token behaviour:**
- Valid for **15 minutes** from issuance
- Single-use — invalidated immediately after the token is redeemed
- Redeeming the token issues a new API key and **invalidates the previous one**

**Local development:** If `RESEND_API_KEY` is not set as a server env var, the recovery URL is printed to the server console instead of being emailed. Use that URL directly to test the full flow without Resend configured.

**Response fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `message` | string | Always `"If that email is registered, a recovery link has been sent."` |

response.name: 200 OK — recovery email sent (or email not found — same response)
500 Internal Server Error — Resend delivery failure

exec: pm.test('New api_key and user_id saved to collection variables', () => {
console.log('API key reset. Old key is now invalid. Store the new one securely.');

variable.description: The UUID token from the recovery email link (the `?token=` query param). Paste it here.

request.description:
Exchanges a valid, unexpired recovery token for a freshly generated API key. The old API key is permanently invalidated and the token is marked as used.

**Authentication:** None required — the token itself is the credential.

**Path parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| `:token` | UUID recovery token from the email link (`?token=<uuid>`). Single-use; expires after 15 minutes. |

**On success:** the test script automatically saves the new `api_key` and `user_id` to collection variables, replacing the old values.

**Response fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `api_key` | string | Your new API key — **shown once only**, save it immediately |
| `id` | string | User UUID |
| `email` | string | Registered email address |
| `name` | string \| null | Display name |

**Errors:**
- `400 Invalid or already-used` — token doesn't exist or was already redeemed
- `400 Expired` — 15-minute window has passed; request a new recovery email

**Frontend flow:** The web UI navigates to `/recover?token=<uuid>` when the email link is clicked. The page calls this endpoint, displays the new key with a copy button, auto-signs the user in, and redirects to the dashboard.

response.name: 400 Bad Request — invalid or already-used token
400 Bad Request — token expired

item.name: Runs
item.description:
Migration run creation and result retrieval.

A run represents a single iPaaS migration or build job.

**Source platform is optional.** Omitting `source` switches Claude to *Build Guide* mode — a step-by-step guide for building the workflow in the destination platform from scratch rather than a migration. The flow is:
1. `POST /api/runs` — create a run (returns `202` immediately, processing is async)
2. `GET /api/runs/:id` — poll every 3–5 seconds until `status` is `completed` or `failed`

**Supported platforms:** `n8n`, `Make`, `Zapier`, `Tray`, `Boomi`, `Workato`, `Celigo`, `Power Automate`

When a run completes, two files are available in the response and in Google Drive:
- **`playbook.md`** — step-by-step migration guide in markdown
- **`flowshift_<src>_to_<dst>.json`** — ready-to-import workflow file for the destination platform (where supported)
### Import file compatibility

| Platform | Import file | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| n8n | ✅ Reliable | Open, well-documented JSON format |
| Make | ✅ Reliable | Blueprint JSON — connection re-linking is expected on any Make import |
| Zapier | ❌ Not available | Zapier doesn't support workflow import; `import_file_content` is always null |
| Tray | ⚠️ Best-effort | Proprietary format — connector configs need manual adjustment |
| Boomi | ⚠️ Best-effort | Complex enterprise schema tied to Atoms/connectors |
| Workato | ⚠️ Best-effort | Proprietary recipe format — trigger/action configs may need adjustment |
| Celigo | ⚠️ Best-effort | Proprietary flows with account-specific mappings and scripts |
| Power Automate | ⚠️ Best-effort | Proprietary Microsoft schema — connections/credentials need manual config |

An amber caveat banner appears in the UI for all ⚠️ platforms when a run completes.

exec: console.log('Run created:', body.id, '— now poll GET /api/runs/:id for results');

request.description:
Start a migration run by describing the source workflow in plain text. No file is required — Claude will use the description to generate the playbook and import file.

**Authentication:** `X-API-Key` header required.

**Request body:**

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `source` | string | | Source platform. Must be one of: `n8n`, `Make`, `Zapier`, `Tray`, `Boomi`, `Workato`, `Celigo`, `Power Automate`. **Optional** — omit to generate a Build Guide instead of a migration playbook. |
| `destination` | string | ✓ | Destination platform. Must be one of: `n8n`, `Make`, `Zapier`, `Tray`, `Boomi`, `Workato`, `Celigo`, `Power Automate`. **Required.** Must differ from `source` if `source` is provided. |
| `description` | string | one of † | Plain-text description of the workflow — triggers, actions, conditions, connected apps. |
| `fileContent` | string | one of † | Full text content of the source workflow file (see *Create Run with file content*). |

† At least one of `description` or `fileContent` must be provided.

Returns `202 Accepted` immediately. Poll `GET /api/runs/:id` for results. Both may be included together.

**Response:** `202 Accepted` with the run object at `status: pending`. The `run_id` is saved automatically by the test script. Processing takes 30–60 seconds — poll *Get Run* until `status` changes.

response.name: 202 Accepted — run queued
response.body:
{
  "id": "09944998-fd18-4f9f-a603-9d87efebddfb",
  "user_id": "7dcaac32-a5dd-4994-9617-113ae5ba98a1",
  "source": "Zapier",
  "destination": "n8n",
  "description": "When a new row is added to Google Sheets, send a Slack message to #general with the row data.",
  "original_filename": null,
  "status": "pending",
  "playbook_text": null,
  "import_file_name": null,
  "import_file_extension": null,
  "import_file_content": null,
  "has_import_file": false,
  "gdrive_run_folder_url": null,
  "error_message": null,
  "created_at": "2026-05-28 17:06:21",
  "completed_at": null
}

response.name: 400 Bad Request — same source and destination
400 Bad Request — no description or file

request.description:
Start a migration run by providing the full text content of the source workflow file. The file is read client-side and sent as a JSON string — no binary upload is involved.

**Authentication:** `X-API-Key` header required.

**Request body:**

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `source` | string | | Source platform. Must be one of: `n8n`, `Make`, `Zapier`, `Tray`, `Boomi`, `Workato`, `Celigo`, `Power Automate`. **Optional** — omit for Build Guide mode. |
| `destination` | string | ✓ | Destination platform. Must be one of: `n8n`, `Make`, `Zapier`, `Tray`, `Boomi`, `Workato`, `Celigo`, `Power Automate`. **Required.** |
| `fileContent` | string | one of † | The complete text content of the workflow file (JSON, YAML, XML, or TXT). Paste the raw file text here. |
| `fileName` | string | with `fileContent` | Original filename including extension (e.g. `my_workflow.json`). Used to name the uploaded file in Google Drive. |
| `description` | string | one of † | Optional plain-text description. Can be combined with `fileContent` for better context. |

† At least one of `fileContent` or `description` must be provided.

**Tip:** To test with a real workflow, open the file in a text editor, copy the entire contents, and paste into `fileContent` as a JSON string (remember to escape quotes and newlines, or use a JSON serialiser).

response.name: 202 Accepted — run queued
response.body:
{
  "id": "1e7054cd-5195-4db9-971a-7634a8af2e12",
  "user_id": "7dcaac32-a5dd-4994-9617-113ae5ba98a1",

  "destination": "Make",

  "gdrive_run_folder_url": null,

  "completed_at": null
}

response.name: 200 OK — completed (poll result)
response.body:
{
  "id": "1e7054cd-5195-4db9-971a-7634a8af2e12",
  "user_id": "7dcaac32-a5dd-4994-9617-113ae5ba98a1",
  "source": "n8n",
  "destination": "Make",
  "description": null,
  "original_filename": "my_workflow.json",
  "status": "completed",
  "playbook_text": "# Flowshift Migration Playbook\n\n## Migration Overview...",
  "import_file_name": "flowshift_n8n_to_make.json",
  "import_file_extension": "json",
  "import_file_content": "{\"name\": \"Migrated Workflow\", \"flow\": [...]}",
  "has_import_file": true,
  "gdrive_run_folder_url": "https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1abc123",
  "error_message": null,
  "created_at": "2026-05-28 17:10:00",
  "completed_at": "2026-05-28 17:10:45"
}

response.name: 400 Bad Request — invalid platform

request.description:
Fetch the full details and results of a specific run. Poll this endpoint every 3–5 seconds after creating a run until `status` reaches `completed` or `failed`.

**Authentication:** `X-API-Key` header required. Users can only fetch their own runs.

**Path parameters:**

| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| `:id` | Run UUID (stored in `{{run_id}}` after Create Run) |

**Status lifecycle:**
```
pending → processing → completed
                    ↘ failed
```

**Response fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | string | Run UUID |
| `user_id` | string | Owning user UUID |
| `source` | string | Source platform |
| `destination` | string | Destination platform |
| `description` | string \| null | Plain-text description, if provided |
| `original_filename` | string \| null | Uploaded filename, if provided |
| `status` | string | `pending` \| `processing` \| `completed` \| `failed` |
| `playbook_text` | string \| null | Full migration playbook in markdown. Populated when `completed`. |
| `import_file_content` | string \| null | Ready-to-import workflow file text. Null if destination doesn't support import (e.g. Zapier). |
| `import_file_name` | string \| null | Suggested filename for the import file (e.g. `flowshift_zapier_to_n8n.json`) |
| `import_file_extension` | string \| null | File extension without dot (e.g. `json`) |
| `has_import_file` | boolean | Convenience flag — true when `import_file_content` is non-null |
| `gdrive_run_folder_url` | string \| null | Google Drive folder URL containing `playbook.md` and the import file. Null if Drive is not authorized. |
| `error_message` | string \| null | Error details when `status` is `failed` |
| `created_at` | string | ISO 8601 UTC timestamp |
| `completed_at` | string \| null | ISO 8601 UTC timestamp. Null while pending or processing. |

**Import file caveats:** n8n and Make produce reliable import files. Tray, Boomi, Workato, Celigo, and Power Automate use proprietary formats — generated files are best-effort approximations that may need manual configuration. Zapier never generates a file (`has_import_file` is always false).

response.name: 200 OK — completed
response.body:
{
  "id": "09944998-fd18-4f9f-a603-9d87efebddfb",
  "user_id": "7dcaac32-a5dd-4994-9617-113ae5ba98a1",
  "source": "Zapier",
  "destination": "n8n",
  "description": "When a new row is added to Google Sheets, send a Slack message to #general.",
  "original_filename": null,
  "status": "completed",
  "playbook_text": "# Flowshift Migration Playbook\n\n## Migration Overview\n...",
  "import_file_name": "flowshift_zapier_to_n8n.json",
  "import_file_extension": "json",
  "import_file_content": "{\"name\": \"Google Sheets → Slack #general\", \"nodes\": [...]}",
  "has_import_file": true,
  "gdrive_run_folder_url": "https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1ILBWqtebGL9GI3kZTp21CBktxmBDqWVI",
  "error_message": null,
  "created_at": "2026-05-28 17:06:21",
  "completed_at": "2026-05-28 17:06:53"
}

item.name: Auth (Google Drive)
item.description:
Google OAuth2 flow for authorizing Drive uploads.

FlowShift uses OAuth2 (not a service account) to write files to your personal Google Drive. This is a one-time browser-based authorization. After you approve, the refresh token is stored in the database and reused automatically for all future runs.

These endpoints are browser-redirect flows — they are not designed to be called from Postman directly (the `GET /auth/google` response is a redirect to Google). They are included here for documentation and reference.

request.description:
Initiates the Google OAuth2 authorization flow. This endpoint redirects the browser to Google's consent screen.

**Authentication:** None required (this IS the authentication flow).

**How to use:**
1. Open `{{base_url}}/auth/google` in a browser (do not use Postman's built-in browser for this)
2. Sign in with the Google account that owns the Drive folder
3. Grant Drive access on the consent screen
4. You will be redirected to `/auth/google/callback`, which stores the refresh token and shows a confirmation page

**Prerequisites:**
- `GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID` and `GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET` must be set as Fly.io secrets
- The GCP OAuth client must have `{{base_url}}/auth/google/callback` as an authorized redirect URI
- Your Google account email must be in the GCP project's test users list (until the app is Google-verified)

**This is a one-time setup.** The refresh token persists across server restarts and redeployments. Re-authorize only if the token is revoked (e.g. you remove Drive access from your Google account).

response.name: 200 OK — after successful authorization (callback)
response.body:
✅ Google Drive authorized

FlowShift can now write playbooks to your Google Drive.

You can close this tab.

response.name: 503 Service Unavailable — OAuth not configured

item.name: Slack (Webhook Receivers)
item.description:
These endpoints are **called by Slack's infrastructure**, not by API consumers directly. They verify Slack's HMAC-SHA256 request signature on every request and reject anything older than 5 minutes.

**Setup:** See `SLACK_SETUP.md` in the repository root for the complete step-by-step guide.

**Required Fly.io secrets:** `SLACK_BOT_TOKEN` (starts with `xoxb-`) and `SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET`.

**User linking:** Users must run `/flowshift link <api-key>` in Slack before running any commands. This maps their Slack user ID to their FlowShift account.

**Result delivery:** When a run completes, FlowShift sends a DM containing a playbook preview, action buttons (View Full Playbook, Open in Drive), and the import file uploaded as a Slack file attachment.

urlencoded.description: Channel where the command was typed
Required to open a modal (valid for 3 seconds)

request.description:
Receives all `/flowshift` slash commands from Slack. Slack posts `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` with a 3-second response deadline — the handler acknowledges with `200 OK` immediately and processes asynchronously.

**Authentication:** HMAC-SHA256 request signature verified against `SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET`. Requests older than 5 minutes are rejected.

**Supported `text` values:**

| `text` value | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| *(empty)* or `new` | Opens the Block Kit migration modal |
| `register` | Opens a registration modal. On submit, reads the user's Slack profile email via `users.info` (`users:read.email` scope), creates a FlowShift account with that email, auto-links the Slack account, and DMs the new API key. Email cannot be overridden — this prevents registering on someone else's behalf. |
| `forgot` | Sends a recovery link to the user's *Slack profile email* (fetched via `users.info`). No email argument accepted — the Slack email is always used. Neutral response regardless of registration status. |
| `link <api-key>` | Links the Slack user to an existing FlowShift account identified by the API key |
| `unlink` | Removes the Slack ↔ FlowShift account mapping |
| `list` | Posts an ephemeral message with the last 5 runs — full UUID, status, timestamp, and a 📂 Drive link per run when a Drive folder exists |
| `status <run-id>` | Posts an ephemeral status for the specified run (first 8 chars work; leading/trailing punctuation from copy-paste is stripped automatically) |
| `help` | Posts the command reference as an ephemeral message |

**Modal fields** (when `text` is empty or `new`):

| Field | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Source Platform | No | `static_select` — leave blank for Build Guide mode |
| Destination Platform | Yes | `static_select` |
| Workflow Description | One of † | `plain_text_input` multiline — max 3,000 chars, markdown supported |
| Workflow File | One of † | `file_input` — JSON, YAML, XML, TXT. Bot downloads it via Slack Files API. |

† At least one must be provided.

**Note:** This endpoint is not designed to be called from Postman. Slack signs every request with a secret your server must verify, so manual calls will always return 401.

Receives interactive component payloads from Slack — modal submissions and button clicks. Slack posts `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` with a `payload` field containing a JSON object.

**Authentication:** Same HMAC-SHA256 signature verification as the commands endpoint.

**Handled payload types:**

| `type` | `callback_id` / `action_id` | Behaviour |
|---|---|---|
| `view_submission` | `flowshift_new_run` | Creates a run from the modal form values. Downloads any uploaded file via Slack Files API, creates the FlowShift run record, sends a *'Processing...'* DM, then processes Claude + Drive upload in the background. When complete, sends a rich DM with playbook preview, action buttons, and the import file as a Slack attachment. |

**Run creation flow on modal submit:**
1. Validate platform inputs
2. Download uploaded file content from Slack (if provided)
3. Open a DM channel with the user
4. Insert run record with `status: pending`
5. Insert `slack_runs` record to link run → DM channel
6. Send *'Generating your playbook...'* DM with run ID
7. Respond `200 OK` (empty body — closes the modal)
8. Process Claude + Drive upload asynchronously in background
9. On completion: send DM with results + upload import file

**Note:** Like the commands endpoint, this is not designed to be called from Postman directly.
